design_brief.goal: Showcase photography work as the primary focus with minimal distraction, making it easy for potential clients to view and contact the photographer
design_brief.audience: Prospective photography clients and art directors seeking to hire or commission work

visual_direction.layout: Centered, grid-based gallery with generous margins and white space. Navigation pinned and subtle. Three-column or masonry grid for photos depending on format mix.
visual_direction.typography: Single modern sans-serif (e.g., Inter, Grotesk). Limited hierarchy: small, restrained body text for navigation and captions; minimal hero text. Let images dominate vertical real estate.
visual_direction.color_usage: Palette is too rich for minimal design. Use white/off-white as primary. Reserve the blue primary (#1e40af) only for interactive states (hover, links, contact button). Retire the secondary and accent colors entirely—they compete with photos.
visual_direction.motion: None, or extremely subtle (fade-in on scroll, no bounce/easing). Animations distract from photography. Keep interactions instant or imperceptibly smooth.
visual_direction.density: Spacious. Every gallery item should breathe. Large vertical gaps between sections. Aim for 40-50% white space on desktop.

content_priorities: Gallery grid (the photography itself is the content)
Clear navigation and artist credibility (brief name/statement)
must_have_sections: Hero (single strong image or minimal text intro with name/tagline)
Gallery grid with photo titles or series grouping
Contact page with simple form or email/social links
Navigation (top bar, minimal, always visible or sticky)
avoid: Carousels or sliders (static grids show more work at once)
Multiple competing colors—gallery competes with brand colors, brand colors lose
Large text blocks or artist biography on home page (link to separate About if needed)
Decorative graphics, shapes, or patterns—they distract from photos
Stock photography that isn't part of the portfolio
Heavy shadows or gradients—keep backgrounds pure white or off-white
Sidebar, complex navigation, or multi-column layouts

tech_recommendations.reason: Content-heavy site benefits from a CMS for easy updates
Already configured — free tier, CDN, instant deploys
Contact forms on static sites need a form backend service
